question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's mirrorRest URL is reachable or returning valid data, causing the readiness probe to pass even when the mirror node REST API is returning 5xx errors and the pinger is operating with a stale network topology, resulting in transfers being routed to incorrect or unavailable nodes and records being missing on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's last successful transfer was within a reasonable time window, causing the liveness probe to pass even when the pinger has been consistently failing to export records for up to 119 seconds (just under the 2-minute heartbeat staleness threshold)?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's transfer ticker is still active, causing the liveness probe to pass even when the ticker has been garbage collected or stopped due to a programming error, resulting in no transfers being submitted and no records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's SDK client has been closed or invalidated, causing the liveness probe to pass even when the client has been closed (e.g., due to a network error or resource exhaustion) and all subsequent transfer attempts will fail, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's goroutines are deadlocked, causing the liveness probe to pass even when the pinger's main goroutine is blocked waiting for a lock or channel that will never be released, and the heartbeat goroutine is still running and updating /tmp/alive, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's transfer receipts are being successfully retrieved (resp.GetReceipt(client)), causing the liveness probe to pass even when transfers are being submitted but receipts are consistently failing, meaning the pinger cannot confirm whether records are being correctly exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck does not check whether the pinger's transfer transaction IDs are being correctly generated, causing the readiness probe to pass even when the SDK is generating duplicate or invalid transaction IDs that will be rejected by consensus nodes, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?
